methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Space & Aerospace (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 23 same-sector peers exist in Space & Aerospace, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Bellatrix Aerospace is a pre-Series B stage space and aerospace company that has raised $20M+ to date. The sector comprises 5 public comparables (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and 8 private peers including SpaceX, Rocket Lab, Blue Origin, Planet Labs, and Firefly Aerospace.

Standard valuation triangulation methods are constrained by data limitations. Peer-median analysis is not applicable; while 23 same-sector peers exist, fewer than three meet narrow filters for stage, adjacency, or valuation tier, making sector-wide medians uninformative. Public-comp anchoring cannot be applied because Bellatrix's revenue is not disclosed—a common limitation for private companies. The Space & Aerospace public peer set carries a median price-to-sales multiple of 45.3x across 6 tickers, but without the target's revenue figure, this anchor cannot be operationalized.

Without revenue data or sufficiently granular peer financial metrics, traditional comparable-company and public-market methods yield no quantitative valuation result. A defensible range would require either: disclosure of Bellatrix's top-line revenue to apply public P/S anchors; identification of at least three private peers at matching stage and tier with known valuations; or recourse to alternative approaches such as venture capital method modeling based on exit assumptions and discount rates. Current data gaps preclude a triangulated valuation estimate at this time.
